evals.name: basic-extraction
evals.prompt: Extract data from this document using the fossil schema. The document is at examples/test_docs/keller_excerpt.md and contains a table with fossil species data.
evals.expected_output: CSV file with fossil_name, geologic_time, geographic_location, water_depth, ecologic_type columns, plus extraction_plan.md and intermediate_data.json

assertions.name: csv_created
assertions.description: Output CSV file exists with correct columns

assertions.name: has_provenance
assertions.description: Extraction plan and intermediate data created

assertions.name: correct_columns
assertions.description: CSV has all schema columns plus document_id

evals.name: schema-inference
evals.prompt: I have a document about shale gas geochemical data. Extract the sample measurements from it. The document is at examples/test_docs/shale_excerpt.md
evals.expected_output: Should either use shale_gas schema or ask user to specify schema. If shale_gas schema used, extract sample_id, depth_m, toc_percent, etc.

assertions.name: schema_selected
assertions.description: Either used built-in schema or asked user to specify

evals.name: context-row-separation
evals.prompt: Extract fossil data from this document. Pay attention: the same species appears in different geological time periods. Document: examples/test_docs/multi_period.md
evals.expected_output: CSV with multiple rows for the same species when it appears in different time periods (context creates separate rows)

assertions.name: context_separation
assertions.description: Same species in different time periods creates separate rows
assertions.check: Verify that if a species appears in multiple time periods, there are multiple rows

assertions.name: row_identity_correct
assertions.description: Row identity combines fossil_name + geologic_time + geographic_location
